content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the white edge boundary visible on the right side, and the car appears to be on the left portion of the track heading southward.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 96, target = 96 + 5 = 101
- Waypoint 101: x=1.0335, y=1.9588
- Car: x=0.8777, y=2.4464
- Bearing = atan2(1.9588 - 2.4464, 1.0335 - 0.8777) = atan2(-0.4876, 0.1558)
- = atan2(-0.4876, 0.1558) → angle ≈ -72.3°
- Current heading: -105.61°
- Delta = -72.3° - (-105.61°) = +33.31° → steer LEFT

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves left ahead (delta +33.31°), car is slightly right of center heading south-southwest, applying left steering at moderate speed to follow waypoint line.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track curves sharply south near waypoints 91-100, then continues south-southwest",
    "Positive delta means steer left (positive steering_angle)",
    "At delta ~33°, use speed=1.5 and steering=+15° to correct",
    "Car is slightly right of center — acceptable, follow waypoints not center",
    "Track width 0.76m — narrow, maintain control through turns",
    "After southward section, waypoints loop back east then north",
    "Avoid overcorrecting — once delta < 5°, reduce steering to 0°",
    "Car heading is around -105° (south-southwest), needs to swing toward -72°",
    "all_wheels_on_track=true — car is stable on track",
    "Progress at 81% — approaching final section of lap, stay smooth"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [0.962, 0.921, 0.878],
    "y": [2.633, 2.539, 2.446],
    "yaw": [-94.8, -100.9, -105.6]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 33.31,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 15.0
}
```
